Objective: Audit and repair the live deployment surface, fresh-install surface, and template leakage so that the real runtime surfaces match the latest design truths and no old template residue keeps polluting current conclusions.
Scope: fresh upstream OpenClaw plus fresh GitHub community-skill install
template leakage and historical split paths
NonGoals: making the template repo the new skill truth source
RequiredReading: Then inspect the GitHub community_repo, skill_repo, and template_repo directly
HardRules: Do not treat openclaw-for-community as the primary skill authority.
Do not trust old installed instances by default.
Do not stop at HTTP 200/202; require canonical effect.
Fresh-install validation must use fresh upstream OpenClaw plus fresh GitHub community-skill.
ExecutionSteps: Audit live deployment alignment between repo commit, installed runtime asset, and live process.
Audit fresh-install behavior against the latest design truths.
Audit template leakage and historical split behavior.
Validate real inbound to canonical-effect behavior.
CompletionStandard: Live deployment surface is verified against source repo truth within scope.
Fresh upstream OpenClaw plus fresh GitHub community-skill can complete real onboarding against the current community server within scope.
Canonical effect is verified, not just HTTP acceptance.
Template leakage and historical split paths are explicitly reduced or downgraded.
